title: Global CSS Variables and Styles
content:
This file defines global CSS variables, animations, and basic body styles for the DocMed application, primarily leveraging Tailwind CSS.

## Purpose

- **Theming:** Establishes a consistent color palette, typography, and border-radius system using CSS variables (HSL format).
- **Animations:** Defines reusable CSS animations like `ripple` and `orbit`.
- **Global Styles:** Applies base styles to `html` and `body` for smooth scrolling, font rendering, and background/foreground colors.
- **Component Styling:** Includes a base style for `.g-button`.

## Key CSS Variables

### Color Palette

- `--background`: Main background color.
- `--foreground`: Primary text and element color.
- `--primary`: Accent color for primary UI elements.
- `--primary-foreground`: Foreground color for primary elements.
- `--muted`: Color for less prominent UI elements.
- `--muted-foreground`: Foreground color for muted elements.
- `--border`: Color for borders.
- `--accent`: Accent color for secondary UI elements.
- `--card`: Background color for cards and containers.
- `--ring`: Color for focus rings.
- `--popover`: Background color for popovers.
- `--popover-foreground`: Foreground color for popovers.
- `--secondary`: Secondary UI color.
- `--secondary-foreground`: Foreground color for secondary elements.
- `--destructive`: Color for destructive actions.
- `--destructive-foreground`: Foreground color for destructive elements.

### Typography & Layout

- `--radius`: Defines the border-radius for elements like buttons.

### Animations

- `--animate-ripple`: CSS animation for a ripple effect.
- `--animate-orbit`: CSS animation for an orbiting effect.

### Shadows

- `--shadow-input`: A shadow style commonly applied to input fields.

## Key Animations

- `@keyframes ripple`: Creates a pulsating ripple effect.
- `@keyframes orbit`: Creates a circular orbiting motion.

## Global Styles

- `*`: Resets `box-sizing` to `border-box`.
- `html`: Enables `smooth` scrolling.
- `body`: Sets the background, foreground, and font family, with font smoothing enabled.

## Component Styles

- `.g-button`:
  - Applies the global `--radius` for rounded corners.
  - Sets a border using the `--btn-border` color.

## Usage Example

To use these variables and styles, you would typically import this CSS file into your main application entry point. For example, in a Next.js application, you might import it in `pages/_app.tsx` or `app/layout.tsx`:

```typescript
// Example: app/layout.tsx (Next.js App Router)
import './globals.css';

export default function RootLayout({ children }: {
  children: React.ReactNode
}) {
  return (
    <html lang="en">
      <body className="bg-background text-foreground">
        {children}
      </body>
    </html>
  );
}
```

In this example, `bg-background` and `text-foreground` would be Tailwind CSS classes that resolve to the CSS variables defined in `globals.css`.

Individual components can also directly utilize these variables:

```css
.my-custom-component {
  background-color: hsl(var(--card));
  border: 1px solid var(--border);
  border-radius: var(--radius);
}
```
